message:
feat(engine): change .js default to ESM, adopt ImportClause enum, and add comprehensive CJS/ESM module interop integration tests

Interpreter behavior change:
- .js files now resolve as ESM (is_cjs=false) instead of CJS, aligning with
  Node.js ESM-by-default semantics. Only .cjs explicitly resolves as CJS.
  The previous behavior incorrectly treated .js as CJS, which broke ESM
  imports from .js files.
- Collapsed nested if-let in ScopeFrame::declare into a single let-chain
  for idiomatic Rust style.

AST import clause migration:
- Updated ast_integration, lowering_coverage_integration, and
  module_compatibility_matrix_integration tests to use the new ImportClause
  enum (ImportClause::Default / ImportClause::SideEffect) introduced in the
  AST layer. The ImportDeclaration struct now carries a typed clause field
  alongside the legacy binding field.
- Cleaned up unused imports in generator_semantics_conformance tests.
- Added DivergenceCategory to module compatibility matrix test imports.

New integration tests (+681 lines in baseline_interpreter_integration):
- require_module_allows_cjs_to_read_esm_default_export: CJS require() of
  an .mjs file can access the default export via mod.default.
- require_module_returns_cjs_module_exports_value: CJS-to-CJS require()
  chain correctly propagates module.exports values.
- require_module_prefers_cjs_over_mjs_for_extensionless_specifier: when
  both dep.cjs and dep.mjs exist, extensionless require('./dep') prefers
  the .cjs variant.
- Additional tests covering circular dependency detection, re-entrant
  require, __filename/__dirname propagation through nested requires, and
  ESM/CJS boundary crossing edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
